Title: User login and session
When: They log in via the Filament admin panel
Then: They are authenticated and redirected to their role-appropriate dashboard
QA Notes: Fortify auth routes respond correctly; invalid credentials show error; session persists across requests; 2FA enforced for roles that require it

Title: Unauthorised access denied
When: They attempt to access any protected route
Then: They are redirected to the login page
QA Notes: All Filament resource routes return 403 for unauthenticated users; no data leaks in redirect responses

Title: Team tenancy data isolation
Given: Two separate teams (tenants) with their own records
When: A user in Team A views any list or record
Then: They see only Team A's data - no records from Team B are visible
QA Notes: Global scope on all tenant-scoped models filters by team_id; direct URL access returns 403 or 404

Title: Role-based permission enforcement
When: They attempt to delete an enquiry
Then: The action is denied (no delete permission for FL role)
QA Notes: Spatie Shield permissions checked via policies; Filament actions hidden/disabled for unauthorised roles; EnquiryPolicy::delete() returns false for FL role

Title: Audit trail on all create/update/delete
Given: Any authenticated user performing a data-changing action
When: They create update or delete a record
Then: An activity log entry is created with user_id action subject_type subject_id properties and timestamp
QA Notes: activity_log table receives entries for all CRUD operations; properties contain changed attributes

Title: Soft delete and restore
When: A user with delete permission deletes the record
Then: The record is soft-deleted (deleted_at set) and restorable by users with restore permission
QA Notes: deleted_at column is set; record no longer appears in default lists; restore action available to MG/AD roles; forceDelete available only to AD

Title: Record locking prevents accidental edits
Given: A service user record where is_locked = true
When: A non-management non-admin user attempts to edit the record
Then: The edit action is denied
QA Notes: PeoplePolicy::update() returns false when is_locked = true for FL/AS/CO/AC roles; MG/AD can still edit

Title: Create enquiry with all required fields
Given: A frontline worker (FL) is logged in
When: They submit a new enquiry with category caller details reason for contact and action taken
Then: The enquiry is saved with status Open and appears in the enquiry list
QA Notes: Enquiry::create() succeeds; status defaults to open; all required fields validated (category people_id or caller name reason_for_contact action_taken user_id occurred_at); Filament form validation rejects missing required fields

Title: Enquiry category selection
When: They select a category from the dropdown
Then: The following categories are available: Family Advice Help for Self School/Madrassa Donation Offer Food Bank Referral Domestic Issues Mental Health Support General Community Support
QA Notes: EnquiryCategory enum values match; Filament select field renders all 8 options with correct labels colors and icons

Title: Enquiry risk and safeguarding flags
Given: A frontline worker creating or editing an enquiry
When: They set safeguarding flags or risk flags
Then: The flags are stored and visually highlighted on the enquiry record
QA Notes: safeguarding_flags (boolean) and risk_flags (text) are saved; Filament renders safeguarding flag with warning/danger indicator; only SG and AD roles see restricted risk details

Title: Enquiry referral tracking
When: The referral type is set to internal or external
Then: The referral destination field is displayed and the referral details are stored
QA Notes: ReferralType enum accepts internal and external; referral_destination is conditionally required when referral_type is set; referral details stored on enquiry record

Title: Convert enquiry to service user
When: A user with ConvertToServiceUser:Enquiry permission clicks Promote to Service User
Then: A new People record with is_service_user = true and a ServiceUserProfile are created; the enquiry status changes to Converted; the original enquiry links to the new service user
QA Notes: ConvertToServiceUser action creates People with is_service_user = true; creates ServiceUserProfile linked to the new People; sets enquiry.status = converted and enquiry.converted_at; links enquiry to service user; audit log entry created

Title: Enquiry status lifecycle
When: It is not converted to a service user
Then: It can be closed with a reason; status transitions are: Open to Converted; Open to Closed
QA Notes: EnquiryStatus enum allows open converted closed; status can be changed manually to Closed; status cannot be changed back from Converted or Closed by FL/AS/CO roles

Title: Enquiry search and filtering
When: A user searches by category status date range or caller name
Then: The list is filtered to show matching enquiries only
QA Notes: Filament table search and filter work for category status occurred_at (date range) and related People name; results are scoped to the current team

Title: Enquiry permissions by role
When: Each role attempts CRUD operations
Then: Permissions enforced per role matrix (FL: Create/View/Update; AS: View/Convert; CO: View; SG: View/Convert; MG: View; AD: All)
QA Notes: Policy checks for each role; Filament hides/shows actions per role; direct HTTP requests return 403 for unauthorised actions

Title: Create service user from enquiry conversion
When: The conversion action completes
Then: A People record is created with is_service_user = true and a ServiceUserProfile is created with all enquiry data transferred
QA Notes: People record has is_service_user = true; ServiceUserProfile linked with person_id; service user accessible via ServiceUserResource; default engagement_status = pending

Title: Create service user directly
When: They create a new service user with required fields (name date of birth target service team)
Then: A People record with is_service_user = true and linked ServiceUserProfile are created
QA Notes: Direct creation via ServiceUserResource; is_service_user set to true; ServiceUserProfile auto-created; required fields validated

Title: Service user profile fields
When: Viewing their case file
Then: The following profile sections are visible: Personal details Contact information Emergency contact Substance use details GP registration Engagement Consent
QA Notes: All fields from ServiceUserProfile and People models render in the case file view; enum fields display labels not values

Title: Service team assignment
When: The target service team is set
Then: The assignment is one of: Assessment Drug & Alcohol Spiritual Education & Outreach Aftercare
QA Notes: ServiceTeam enum values are the only valid options; assignment determines which counselor types can access the record

Title: Engagement status lifecycle
Given: A service user with engagement status Pending
When: Their engagement changes
Then: Status can transition through: Pending to Active to Inactive to Discharged
QA Notes: EngagementStatus enum allows all four states; status changes are audit-logged; status transitions visible in case file timeline

Title: Consent tracking
When: Consent fields are set (data storage referrals communications)
Then: Each consent field is a boolean that is explicitly captured and visible on the profile
QA Notes: consent_data_storage consent_referrals consent_communications are boolean fields; default to false; must be explicitly set; displayed as checkboxes/toggles in Filament form

Title: Service user permissions by role
Given: Various roles performing service user actions
When: Each role attempts CRUD operations
Then: Permissions enforced per role matrix (FL: View limited; AS: Create/View/Update assigned; CO: Create/View/Update assigned; SG: Create/View/Update all; MG: View all; AD: All)
QA Notes: ServiceUserPolicy enforces role-based access; assigned means service user's target_service_team matches user's counselor_types or department

Title: Create a standard note
Given: A user with Create:Note permission (FL AS CO SG)
When: They create a note attached to a People or Company record
Then: The note is saved with title body author timestamp and visibility = standard
QA Notes: Note created with creator_id = auth()->id() visibility = standard team_id = current_team_id; timestamp auto-set; note appears on the related record's notes relation manager

Title: Create a restricted note
When: They create a note with visibility = restricted
Then: The note is saved and visible only to safeguarding leads and admins
QA Notes: Note created with visibility = restricted; NotePolicy::view() returns false for non-SG/AD users; restricted notes hidden from FL/AS/CO/AC/FR/MG in Filament queries; restricted note creation UI only visible to SG/AD

Title: Restricted note access enforcement
Given: A restricted note on a service user record
When: A non-safeguarding user (FL AS CO AC FR MG) attempts to view it
Then: The note is not visible in any list detail view or API response
QA Notes: Global scope on Note model filters visibility = restricted for non-SG/AD roles; direct URL /admin/notes/{id} returns 403 for non-SG/AD; audit log records the access attempt

Title: Note audit trail on access
When: A safeguarding lead views it
Then: An audit log entry is created recording the access
QA Notes: Activity log entry created with event = viewed subject_type = Note subject_id = note_id causer_id = user_id; includes timestamp

Title: Note polymorphic attachment
When: They attach the note to a People record or a Company record
Then: The note is linked via the noteables pivot and appears on the respective record
QA Notes: noteables pivot stores note_id noteable_type noteable_id; note appears in the People resource notes relation manager; note appears in the Company resource notes relation manager

Title: Note timestamps and author
When: Viewing the note detail or list
Then: The created_at timestamp and creator name are displayed
QA Notes: created_at and creator_id are auto-populated; Filament columns show creator.name and formatted created_at

Title: Note permissions by role
When: Each role attempts CRUD operations on standard and restricted notes
Then: Permissions enforced per role matrix (FL/AS/CO: Create Standard/View Standard/Edit Own; SG: Create All/View All/Edit Own+Restricted; MG: View Standard only; AD: All)
QA Notes: Note creation form shows visibility field only for SG/AD; NotePolicy enforces view and create permissions per role; edit/delete limited to own notes unless SG/AD

Title: Create an appointment
When: They book an appointment selecting service user counselor type date start/end time
Then: The appointment is created in the Schedule system with status scheduled
QA Notes: Schedule created with schedule_type = appointment; schedulable_type = User (counselor); metadata includes service_user_id counselor_type payment_type; status defaults to scheduled (AppointmentStatus enum)

Title: Appointment type classification
When: They select the appointment type
Then: The type must be one of: Assessment Drug & Alcohol Spiritual Education & Outreach Aftercare
QA Notes: Metadata field counselor_type stores a valid CounselorType enum value; appointment visible to counselors matching that specialty

Title: Counselor availability validation
Given: A counselor with defined availability schedules
When: A user books an appointment outside the counselor's available hours
Then: The system warns or prevents the booking
QA Notes: Schedule system checks schedule_type = availability for the counselor on the selected date; overlapping or out-of-availability appointments trigger validation error or warning

Title: Email confirmation on booking
When: The booking is saved
Then: An email confirmation is sent to the service user (if email provided) and/or the assigned counselor
QA Notes: SendAppointmentReminders or similar notification fires; Mailable/Notification class sends to relevant recipients; email includes date time counselor name location and cancellation link

Title: Appointment status transitions
When: Status changes occur
Then: Valid transitions are: scheduled to confirmed to completed; scheduled to cancelled; confirmed to cancelled
QA Notes: AppointmentStatus enum defines valid states; Filament action buttons for status transitions; audit log records each status change

Title: Appointment permissions by role
When: Each role attempts CRUD operations
Then: Permissions enforced per role matrix (FL: Create/View; AS: View assigned; CO: View/Update/Cancel own; SG: View all; MG: Create/View/Update/Cancel/Delete all; AD: All)
QA Notes: SchedulePolicy enforces per-role permissions; own means schedulable_id = auth()->id(); assigned means service user is in user's assigned team

Title: Shared diary view
Given: A user with view appointment permissions
When: They access the diary/calendar view
Then: They see all appointments they are authorised to view in a calendar or list format
QA Notes: Filament calendar/list widget shows appointments scoped to user's access level; FL sees all bookings; CO sees own availability + booked appointments; MG sees all

Title: Log a check-in call
When: They log a telephone check-in for a service user
Then: A task record is created with type = Follow-up Call linked to the service user with outcome and next steps
QA Notes: Task created with type = follow_up_call taskable_type = People taskable_id = service_user_id; includes due_date assigned to AC user; visible in service user's case file under related tasks

Title: Follow-up task auto-generation - Active users
Given: A service user with engagement_status = Active
When: A check-in call is logged
Then: A follow-up task is auto-generated with due date = 1 week or 2 weeks (configurable) from the call date
QA Notes: Follow-up task created with type = follow_up_call due date set per configured interval; task assigned to the aftercare team/worker; creation logged in audit trail

Title: Follow-up task auto-generation - Post-care users
Given: A service user with engagement_status = Inactive (post-care sober less than 2 years)
When: A check-in call is logged
Then: A monthly follow-up task is auto-generated
QA Notes: Follow-up task created with monthly interval for inactive/post-care users; system differentiates between Active (weekly/bi-weekly) and Inactive (monthly) schedules

Title: Due/overdue task alerts
Given: Follow-up tasks with approaching or past due dates
When: A task is due today or overdue
Then: The task appears in an alert/dashboard widget for aftercare workers and managers
QA Notes: Task list filtered by due_date less than or equal to today and status not equal to completed; Filament widget or notification shows due/overdue tasks; MG can view all overdue tasks across teams

Title: Engagement history on service user record
Given: A service user with logged check-ins
When: Viewing the service user case file
Then: All check-in calls follow-ups outcomes and next steps are visible in chronological order
QA Notes: Task relation manager on ServiceUserResource shows related tasks with type follow_up_call; tasks ordered by created_at descending; each task shows outcome next steps and assigned worker

Title: Aftercare permissions by role
When: Each role attempts check-in and follow-up operations
Then: Permissions enforced per role matrix (AC: Create/View assigned/Update own; SG: View all; MG: View all/Update; AD: All)
QA Notes: TaskPolicy enforces per-role check-in and follow-up permissions; AC can only create tasks with type = follow_up_call for assigned service users

Title: Partner organisation directory
Given: A user with directory access (CO SG MG AD)
When: They access the partner directory
Then: They can create view search and export partner organisation records with name address contact details type of support offered referral criteria notes and referral history
QA Notes: Company model with type = partner or custom field identifying directory type; Filament CompanyResource with partner-specific fields; search by name and type; export functionality available to MG/AD

Title: Education & outreach directory
When: They access the education directory
Then: They can create view search and export records for madrassas schools colleges and youth organisations including key contacts email addresses engagement history
QA Notes: Company model with type = education or custom field; key contacts stored as related People records; engagement history via notes relation; searchable by name and type

Title: Donor & supporter directory
When: They access the donor directory
Then: They can create view search and export donor/supporter records
QA Notes: People records with type indicating donor role; FR can view/create donors; FR cannot see other record types; AD has full access; donor search and export available

Title: Directory export
Given: A user with export permission (MG AD)
When: They click export on a directory
Then: The directory data is exported as a downloadable file (CSV or Excel)
QA Notes: Filament export action generates downloadable file; export includes all visible fields; export scoped to current team

Title: Directory permissions by role
When: Each role attempts directory operations
Then: Permissions enforced per role matrix (FL: Read Partners/Education; CO: Read/Write Partners/Education; AC: Read Partners/Education; FR: Read/Write Donors only; MG: Read all; AD: Full)
QA Notes: CompanyPolicy and PeoplePolicy enforce per-role directory access; navigation menu items hidden for roles without access; direct URL access returns 403

Title: Record a donation
When: They create a donation record with donor amount date and Gift Aid status
Then: The donation is saved and linked to the donor (People or Company)
QA Notes: Donation record created with donor_type donor_id amount donated_at gift_aid_status; polymorphic relation to People/Company; Gift Aid status is a selectable field

Title: Gift Aid consent tracking
Given: A donor record with Gift Aid consent
When: Viewing the donor profile or donation list
Then: The Gift Aid status and consent are clearly displayed
QA Notes: gift_aid_status field on Donation or People model; consent fields visible on donor profile; donations eligible for Gift Aid are filterable

Title: Donation reporting by period and donor
When: A manager (MG) or admin (AD) runs a donation report
Then: Donations can be filtered and grouped by date range donor and Gift Aid status
QA Notes: Filament table filters for donated_at date range donor_id gift_aid_status; aggregate totals available; export to CSV/Excel

Title: Donation permissions by role
When: Each role attempts CRUD operations
Then: Permissions enforced per role matrix (FR: Create/View/Update own team/Report; MG: View all/Report; AD: All)
QA Notes: Donation policy restricts access per role; FR cannot delete; MG read-only with report access; AD has full access

Title: Create a safeguarding flag
When: They create a safeguarding flag on a service user or enquiry
Then: The flag is stored with severity details and status and is visible only to safeguarding leads and admins
QA Notes: SafeguardingFlag record created with service_user_id enquiry_id (optional) severity details status = open created_by_id; only visible to SG and AD via global scope or policy

Title: Safeguarding flag visibility restriction
Given: A safeguarding flag on a service user
When: A non-safeguarding user (FL AS CO AC FR MG) views the service user record
Then: Safeguarding flags are not visible
QA Notes: SafeguardingFlag model has a global scope filtering for SG/AD roles; Filament relation manager hidden for non-SG/AD; direct API access returns empty result for non-SG/AD

Title: Resolve a safeguarding flag
When: A safeguarding lead resolves it with notes
Then: The flag status changes to resolved with resolved_at timestamp and resolving user
QA Notes: Status transition from open to resolved; resolved_at and resolved_by (optional) recorded; audit log entry created; resolved flags visible but clearly marked

Title: Safeguarding flag on enquiry
Given: A frontline worker creating an enquiry
When: They tick the safeguarding flag checkbox
Then: A safeguarding flag is created and an alert is sent to safeguarding leads
QA Notes: enquiry.safeguarding_flags = true triggers flag creation; notification sent to SG role users; flag linked to both the enquiry and the associated person

Title: Restricted notes visibility (cross-ref NT-002/003)
Given: See Notes module - NT-002 and NT-003
QA Notes: Verified under NT-002 and NT-003

Title: Consent tracking on service user (cross-ref SU-006)
QA Notes: Verified under SU-006

Title: Consent field visibility
Given: A service user profile with consent fields
When: Viewing the profile
Then: Consent fields (data storage referrals communications) are prominently displayed with clear yes/no indicators
QA Notes: Filament form shows consent fields as toggles/checkboxes with clear labels; consent status visible in service user list view or summary; changes to consent are audit-logged

Title: Enquiry volume and category report
When: They request an enquiry report for a date range
Then: They see count of enquiries by category status and handling team member
QA Notes: Filament report or widget showing aggregate enquiry data; filterable by occurred_at date range category status; data scoped to current team

Title: Service user engagement report
When: They request an engagement report
Then: They see service user count by service team engagement status and date range
QA Notes: Report showing target_service_team distribution and engagement_status counts; filterable by date range; scoped to current team

Title: Donation and Gift Aid report
Given: A fundraising (FR) manager (MG) or admin (AD) user
When: They request a donation report
Then: They see donation totals by period donor and Gift Aid status
QA Notes: Donation report with totals and Gift Aid breakdown; exportable to CSV/Excel; scoped to current team or donor

Title: Export functionality
Given: Any user with export permission on a resource
When: They click the export button on a list view
Then: The data is exported as a downloadable file (CSV or Excel)
QA Notes: Filament export action generates file; export respects current filters and team scope; file contains all visible columns; large exports are queued

Title: Report access permissions
When: Each role attempts to view and export reports
Then: Permissions enforced per role matrix (FL: None; SG: Read enquiries; FR: Read/Write donations; MG: Read all/Export; AD: Read/Write all/Export)
QA Notes: Dashboard widgets and report pages restricted per role; export button visibility matches permission matrix

Title: Automatic logging of model changes
When: They create update or delete an Enquiry People ServiceUserProfile Note Company Task or Schedule
Then: An activity log entry is created with the user action subject and changed properties
QA Notes: activity_log table has entries for each CRUD action; entry includes causer_id subject_type subject_id description properties (old/new values) created_at; Spatie ActivityLog package configured for all relevant models

Title: Access logging for restricted notes
Given: A safeguarding lead viewing a restricted note
When: They access the note
Then: The access is logged with timestamp and user identity
QA Notes: Activity log entry with event = viewed for restricted note access; includes causer_id and subject_id

Title: Audit log access permissions
When: They navigate to the audit log
Then: They can view all activity log entries filtered by user model action and date range
QA Notes: Filament ActivityLog resource accessible to MG/AD roles only; filterable by causer_id subject_type event created_at; entries cannot be edited or deleted by any role except AD

Title: Audit log is read-only
When: They view the audit log
Then: They cannot create update or delete log entries manually
QA Notes: No create/update/delete actions in ActivityLog resource; direct API requests return 403; only system-generated entries exist

Title: Page load time
Given: A standard dataset (10000 records or fewer per model)
When: A user loads any Filament resource list page
Then: The page renders within 3 seconds
QA Notes: Measure with browser DevTools or Lighthouse; average response time less than 3s for list pages with pagination

Title: Enquiry creation speed
When: They log a new phone enquiry
Then: The entire process (open form fill submit) takes under 60 seconds
QA Notes: UAT criterion from kickoff checklist; form has minimal required fields; auto-fill where possible (caller lookup timestamp handler)

Title: Data isolation between tenants
Given: Team A and Team B with separate data
When: Any user in Team A performs any read or write operation
Then: They never see or modify Team B's data
QA Notes: All tenant-scoped models use global scope filtering by team_id; direct URL manipulation returns 404/403; no cross-tenant data leaks in API or Filament responses

Title: HTTPS enforcement
When: A user accesses the application
Then: All requests are served over HTTPS
QA Notes: SSL/TLS certificate installed; HTTP requests redirect to HTTPS; Herd secure site or production server configured

Title: Session security
When: The session is inactive for the configured timeout
Then: The session expires and the user must re-authenticate
QA Notes: Session lifetime configured in config/session.php; Filament auth session respects timeout; idle sessions expire

Title: Browser support
When: They use Chrome Edge Safari or Firefox (latest 2 versions)
Then: All features function correctly
QA Notes: UAT testing on Chrome Edge Safari Firefox latest; no JavaScript errors; all Filament components render correctly
